groups.verdict_summary: Transit's home (apps/web/src/routes/[[lang=locale]]/+page.svelte) is a single-screen "command board" — one Surface stacking a two-column hero (Masthead + live-pulse TerminalPanel), a quiet "What this is" prose block, and a launchpad tile grid. It is honest, competent, and well-composed as a dashboard directory — but it has NONE of yesid's home THEATRE: no scroll journey, no metro-draw intro, no color takeover, no thesis headline (it uses the agency name as h1), no live SQL terminal + PULL FRESH DATA, no manifesto beat, no vertical edge-titles, no hazard-tape rhythm, and no climactic conversion CTA. yesid's home is a 7-movement scroll narrative (HomePage.svelte) that OPENS on an 800%-pinned metro-network draw ending in a full-orange takeover into a two-line thesis + live SQL panel. The single biggest move: rebuild the home as a scroll-driven JOURNEY — pinned metro-draw → color takeover → thesis hero with the live network terminal — and demote today's command board to the journey's honest reduced-motion fallback + closing directory movement. The substrate exists (gsap dep, TerminalPanel, Masthead, live store with .refresh(), blueprint grid, hazard Separator); what's missing is the composition on top.
keeps: Honest-absence on the live pulse KPIs is exemplary and AT PARITY with yesid's own honesty pass — MetricDisplay with absentReason='not-reported'/emptyLabel, null never a fabricated 0, verdict WORD off the reliabilityVerdict floors (+page.svelte:583-612). Keep this exact honesty contract for the new live SQL terminal.

TerminalPanel chassis (brand/TerminalPanel.svelte) — signal-head titlebar, hazard stripe, mono footer, sanctioned cursorGlow, SOLID --surface-2 occlusion — is the correct transit-native analog of yesid's terminal chrome. Reuse it verbatim as the SQL-terminal shell (do not restyle).

Full EN+FR provider-agnostic copy templating on short_name/city/display_name (+page.svelte:82-83,175-228) — at parity with yesid's bilingual content and honest about provider. Keep; extend the same pattern to the new thesis/manifesto/terminal copy.

The Masthead identity family (kicker → display title + orange dot → lede) with CornerMeta blueprint-margin annotations (+page.svelte:450-474) is strong hero-left DNA and maps 1:1 to yesid's HeroTextContent. Keep it as the thesis chassis (swap the h1 source — see gap).

The launchpad IA — surfaces grouped Explore / Accountability / Trust with weighted feature-vs-compact tiles (+page.svelte:306-438) — is genuinely useful wayfinding yesid's home lacks. Keep it, but relocate it to the journey's CLOSING 'terminus' movement (yesid HomePage.svelte:304 Closer), not the hero.

gaps.element: Scroll journey / metro-draw intro / color takeover — the entire opening theatre
gaps.yesid_evidence: HomePage.svelte:228-314 is a 7-section scroll narrative; HeroBanner.svelte:1-19 documents the pinned intro: Phase 1 'NEXT STOP: SCROLL DOWN' billboard + Berri-UQAM dot, Phase 2 metro lines DrawSVG outward, Phase 5 zoom into the node 'fills viewport with orange', Phase 6 cross-fade into the orange thesis container. 800% desktop pin (line 266), Lenis smooth-scroll, DrawSVG + CustomEase (line 241).
gaps.transit_state: +page.svelte renders ONE <Surface> with three stacked static sections — zero scroll scenes, zero pin, zero draw. gsap.ts (motion/utils/gsap.ts) registers ONLY SplitText and explicitly says ScrollTrigger/DrawSVG are NOT pulled in. The whole opening theatre is absent.
gaps.redo_spec: Build features/home/HeroJourney.svelte as a scroll-pinned scene. (1) Add a lazy ScrollTrigger+DrawSVG+CustomEase loader to motion/utils/gsap.ts (mirror yesid loadDrawSVG/loadCustomEase; keep the chrome bundle lean via dynamic import). (2) Ship a transit-native network SVG (a schematic line drawing of the provider's real trunk routes — ornament, aria-hidden, NOT presented as data) as static/images/network-schematic.svg, drawn with a traveling --primary dot. (3) Timeline: 'NEXT STOP: SCROLL DOWN' / 'PROCHAIN ARRÊT : FAIS DÉFILER' mono billboard (font-mono black, --primary) → lines DrawSVG outward → zoom a node to a full-viewport --primary takeover → cross-fade into the thesis hero (gap 2). Reduced-motion + PRM: gate the entire pin behind isPrefersReducedMotion() (motion/reduced-motion.svelte.ts) and render today's static command board instead — the scroll-honest fallback the operator requires. Data honesty: the metro art is decorative geometry, never a live claim; all live numbers still come from the store.

gaps.element: Thesis headline — the two-line display statement instead of the agency name
gaps.yesid_evidence: HeroTextContent.svelte:66-101: h1 is a two-line display thesis ('SYSTEMS THAT DON'T BREAK.') at text-hero, line2 in --primary, terminated by an SVG orange-dot period that is the zoom center. It is a POINT OF VIEW, not a label.
gaps.transit_state: +page.svelte:453 sets the hero h1 to heading={manifest.display_name} — literally the agency name. This is exactly the 'agency-name h1' the operator flagged. The Masthead already supports an orange dot but carries no thesis.
gaps.redo_spec: Feed the Masthead heading a two-line THESIS (keep the existing orange-dot terminator, brand/Masthead.svelte), demote manifest.display_name to the kicker/CornerMeta. Bilingual options (line1 / line2, EN // FR): (A) 'THE NETWORK, / MEASURED HONESTLY.' // 'LE RÉSEAU, / MESURÉ HONNÊTEMENT.'  (B) 'NUMBERS THAT / DON'T LIE.' // 'DES CHIFFRES / QUI NE MENTENT PAS.'  (C) 'EVERY BUS, / ACCOUNTED FOR.' // 'CHAQUE BUS, / RENDU DES COMPTES.' — (A) is the strongest transit-voice analog of yesid's 'don't break' honesty thesis and reuses the site's own 'we never invent data' spine (already in tagline, +page.svelte:180,205). Line2 in --primary; period = the existing Masthead dot as the takeover zoom target.

gaps.element: Live SQL terminal + PULL FRESH DATA — the signature interaction
gaps.yesid_evidence: HeroSqlPanel.svelte:43-91 renders a real syntax-highlighted Gold-layer SELECT…GROUP BY route ORDER BY vehicles + a live results table (route / avg_delay_s / vehicles) fed from live transit data; HeroBanner.svelte:615-627 is the 'PULL FRESH DATA' refresh button (styled --primary gradient, NOT the reserved amber) that refetches and spins.
gaps.transit_state: Transit has a live-pulse TerminalPanel but it shows only 4 KPI tiles — NO query text, NO row results table, NO user-triggered refresh. Ironic: yesid renders this by PROXYING transit.yesid.dev, while transit owns the raw feed directly and doesn't show it.
gaps.redo_spec: Build features/home/LiveNetworkTerminal.svelte inside a TerminalPanel: (1) a static syntax-highlighted query (reuse yesid's --primary/--terminal-ink/--accent-text span coloring) reading e.g. `SELECT route_short_name, round(avg(abs(delay_min)),1) AS avg_delay, count(vehicle_id) FROM live.vehicles GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 3 DESC LIMIT 5`; (2) a live results table computed CLIENT-SIDE from the store's live.vehicles by faceting aggregateLive (v1/live/aggregate.ts) per route_short_name — real live rows, honest-null when a lane is missing; (3) a 'PULL FRESH DATA' / 'ACTUALISER LES DONNÉES' button wired to the existing live.refresh() (store.svelte.ts:86) with a spin on the ↻ glyph, styled --primary gradient like yesid's refresh-btn (NOT amber — amber stays the one map conversion CTA). Keep the FreshnessStamp + StatusDot live badge already present. This is the transit-native equivalent the prompt asked for: our /v1 feed, queried live, refreshable.

gaps.element: Manifesto beat — full-viewport color-takeover thesis movement
gaps.yesid_evidence: Manifesto.svelte is a full-100dvh beat (line 216) on --manifesto ground with a circuit-grid schematic (line 230), a giant display statement (.manifesto__line-huge clamp up to 11rem, --primary, line 301-312), a terminal prompt, capability pills, and a live countdown — a dedicated theatre movement between hero and services.
gaps.transit_state: Transit's equivalent is +page.svelte:534-553 'What this is' — a quiet muted-foreground prose paragraph + 3 small pillar cards. Informative but flat: no full-viewport moment, no display-scale statement, no color takeover, no schematic.
gaps.redo_spec: Promote 'What this is' to features/home/HonestyManifesto.svelte — a full-viewport beat on the blueprint-grid substrate (P5.4 grid tokens already exist site-wide). Compose: a huge display line carrying the honesty thesis ('WHEN A NUMBER IS MISSING, / WE SHOW IT MISSING.' // 'QUAND UNE DONNÉE MANQUE, / ON L'AFFICHE ABSENTE.') at clamp display scale in --primary; a mono terminal prompt line above (reuse TerminalCursor pattern); the existing 3 honesty pillars (Live/Honest/Accountable, +page.svelte:250-275) restyled as station-voice pills (SectionLabel variant='station'). Keep the /metrics 'How we measure' link as the pill row's anchor. This is the narrative bridge yesid uses; it's non-data so scroll-theatre is legal here.

gaps.element: Vertical edge-titles on home sections
gaps.yesid_evidence: HomePage.svelte:255,266,290,305 — each titled section carries a rotated SectionHeading (home-section--right / --left) alternating side per movement (Services R → About L → Projects R → Terminus L); CSS at :325-360 (writing-mode vertical-rl, sticky top:50%, computed fit). Operator ruling #2 re-legalized these.
gaps.transit_state: Transit home has zero rotated titles — every section uses a flat horizontal SectionHeading/SectionLabel. P5.4a deleted edge-letters on the premise 'yesid retired them', which the operator flagged as WRONG.
gaps.redo_spec: Give each home MOVEMENT (Manifesto, LiveTerminal, Launchpad/Terminus) a rotated vertical edge-title, alternating L/R, reusing the existing SectionHeading primitive with a rotated-title wrapper (port yesid's .rotated-title CSS block, HomePage.svelte:325-413: vertical-rl, sticky top:50%, --left rotate 180deg, hidden < 1024px with a horizontal mobile fallback). Skip the computeRotatedTitleSize util for v1 — a font-size: min(clamp(3.5rem,7vw,6rem),5.5dvh) is adequate. Titles: 'MANIFESTE/MANIFESTO', 'RÉSEAU/NETWORK', 'TERMINUS'.

gaps.element: Section rhythm — hazard-tape between movements + numbered stations
gaps.yesid_evidence: HomePage.svelte:233,240,260,277,301 place <Separator variant='hazard'> between EVERY section — a hazard-tape spine that paces the scroll. yesid also uses numbered/schematic ornament throughout.
gaps.transit_state: +page.svelte has exactly ONE hazard Separator (line 531) between hero and 'What this is'; the launchpad groups butt together with no dividers. The NumberedChip primitive (brand barrel) is unused on home — no 01/02/03 movement rhythm.
gaps.redo_spec: Insert <Separator variant='hazard'> between each journey movement (hero → manifesto → live terminal → terminus), matching yesid's cadence. Number the movements with the existing NumberedChip (brand/NumberedChip.svelte) on each SectionHeading — 01 Manifesto / 02 Network / 03 Explore — so the scroll has station-count rhythm. Optionally wire SectionProgress (already in the brand barrel) as a journey rail. All existing primitives; no new components.

gaps.element: Schematic / draftsman background ornament layer
gaps.yesid_evidence: HomePage.svelte:246-248 mounts ServicesBlueprint as a full-width -z-10 background behind the services section; Manifesto.svelte:230-249 layers a circuit-grid + solder-dot schematic. The home has a persistent technical-drawing texture layer.
gaps.transit_state: Transit home renders on the flat blueprint-grid substrate but adds NO dedicated schematic ornament — no scale marks, no draftsman linework, no circuit layer behind any movement. The hero and 'What this is' sit on plain ground.
gaps.redo_spec: Add a home schematic ornament layer behind the Manifesto and/or hero movement — a -z-10 absolute layer of --primary-tinted repeating-linear-gradient rules + solder-dot radial-gradients (port Manifesto.svelte:230-249 verbatim; it already uses color-mix on --primary and respects reduced-motion). gaps.element: Climactic conversion CTA — the journey has no destination action
gaps.yesid_evidence: HomePage.svelte:297-299 places a CtaBand mid-journey carrying the ONE reserved conversion action (yesid: 'Book a call', amber ground); HeroTextContent.svelte:139 uses variant='conversion' for that single amber CTA.
gaps.transit_state: Transit home has NO conversion CTA at all. The reserved amber 'Stops near me' lives only on /map. Every launchpad tile is equal-weight --primary (+page.svelte:901-926) — the journey ends in a flat directory with no climactic beat.
gaps.redo_spec: Give the home its ONE per-view conversion moment (the hard-law amber-ground exception): a CTA band at the journey's terminus — 'Open the live map' / 'Ouvrir la carte en direct' routing to /map (openSurface({kind:'map'})), styled amber ground (the single reserved conversion CTA for this view, exactly as the law permits one per view). Keep all launchpad tiles --primary. This turns the closing directory into a destination with a clear primary action, matching yesid's mid-journey CtaBand rhythm.

groups.notes: Substrate confirmed present, so every redo spec is buildable on HEAD: gsap 3.15 is a dep (package.json) though only SplitText is registered (motion/utils/gsap.ts) — ScrollTrigger/DrawSVG/CustomEase need a lazy loader added, exactly the 'add a lazy loader when a scroll surface needs one' TODO that file already anticipates. @yesid/motion is vendored (cursorGlow used by TerminalPanel). The live store already exposes .refresh(): Promise<void> (store.svelte.ts:86) and live.vehicles, so PULL FRESH DATA + a live results table need no new data plumbing. Reduced-motion is first-class (isPrefersReducedMotion, motion/reduced-motion.svelte.ts) — the CURRENT static command board is a ready-made scroll-honest fallback; spec every scroll scene to collapse to it under PRM (yesid does the same, HeroBanner.svelte:696-723). Honesty guardrails to carry into P5-R home: (a) the metro/network SVG is decorative geometry, never presented as live positions; (b) the live SQL results table must render honest-absence (MetricDisplay absentReason) per lane, never a fabricated row — reuse the pulse KPI contract already in +page.svelte:583-612; (c) PRM silence — no auto-advancing countdown unless IO-gated like yesid's Manifesto (Manifesto.svelte:123-149). Group boundary: 'projects' edge-title verification the prompt mentioned belongs to the projects lane; for HOME the vertical-edge-title grammar is proven by yesid HomePage.svelte:255/266/290/305 (rotated section titles), which is sufficient to re-legalize them here.

groups.verdict_summary: The map is the kinetic flagship — the live canvas, motion controller, encoding doctrine and full-bleed/overlay laws are genuinely at parity and must be kept. But every piece of CHROME floating over it is bespoke, generic-card CSS that composes ZERO brand primitives. TerminalPanel, CornerMarks, CornerMeta, Masthead, StatusDot signal-head, and hazard Separator all exist in lib/components/brand/ and the map imports none of them (verified: grep for those names across features/map/*.svelte returns nothing). yesid's brand IS the terminal-window chassis (HeroSqlPanel, TerminalPanel: 2px --border-rule frame + signal-head titlebar + hazard stripe + mono footer readout) plus draftsman schematic ornament (ProjectsBlueprint, Beck-style route lines) plus coordinate/corner-mark framing. The map reads as a competent Mapbox clone with brand tokens sprinkled on, not as the same draftsman brand as yesid home. Single biggest move: re-chassis all four overlay panels (title, near-me, filter, detail) onto TerminalPanel + wrap the canvas in CornerMarks + a coordinate CornerMeta readout, so the live map reads as a control-room terminal, not a generic map widget.
keeps: Full-bleed canvas law + 13-layer absolute-overlay architecture (MapHero.svelte:1168-1252, mapZ ladder) — panels never resize the GL context. This is correct and load-bearing; P5-R composes ON it.

The ONE amber conversion CTA is correctly placed and colored: near-me toggle rides --accent ground / --accent-foreground ink, 44px, exactly one per view (MapNearMeControl.svelte:307-325). Keep the color+placement; only re-chassis the panel it opens.

Encoding doctrine (one color per entity type, state→combinable filter that repaints+hides) and the live forward-projection motion controller (MapHero.svelte:942-997) — the kinetic soul of the surface. Frozen marks; do not touch.
URL-driven filter spine (createFilterStore + goto replaceState, MapHero.svelte:212-231) makes every view deep-linkable — matches yesid's shareable-state grammar. Keep.

Honest-absence discipline: liveEdgeState / feed-stall banner / per-bus not-reporting note are all real-signal-only, no fabricated data (MapHero.svelte:414-427, 482-483). Brand-correct; keep the logic, only restyle the vessels.

gaps.element: Overlay panels use generic rounded-card CSS instead of the TerminalPanel chassis — the single defining brand element yesid uses everywhere
gaps.yesid_evidence: HeroSqlPanel.svelte:43-46 (rounded-lg border-2 border-[--border-rule] bg-[--terminal] + prompt + pulsing StatusDot 'live' header) and TerminalPanel.svelte:72-119 (signal-head titlebar → hazard Separator → body → mono footer readout) — the terminal window is THE yesid idiom.
gaps.transit_state: MapNearMeControl.svelte:358-373 (.map-near-panel) is a bespoke color-mix card + blur, no titlebar/signal-head/hazard/footer. MapOverlayChrome hover peek .map-peek:194-210 same. The detail RightPanel and filter panel likewise generic. Zero use of TerminalPanel (grep confirmed) though it exists at lib/components/brand/TerminalPanel.svelte.
gaps.redo_spec: Re-vessel every floating panel as <TerminalPanel>. Near-me: title='stm@live:near-me>' tag='GEO', body = the search form, footer readout = origin + nearest-N. Filter panel: title='filter:state>' with the chips in the body, footerItems = active-count. Hover peek: compact TerminalPanel (noGlow, noPadding). All get the signal-head (StatusDot green pulse + caution/stop @25%), the hazard Separator stripe, 2px --border-rule frame, --surface-2 solid (occlusion law). This alone flips the map from 'map widget' to 'yesid control room'. Keep the amber CTA toggle as-is; only its opened panel becomes a TerminalPanel.

gaps.element: Title block is bare kicker+heading floating text — no Masthead grammar, no signal head, no hazard tape, and it uses a forbidden text-shadow
gaps.yesid_evidence: Masthead.svelte:92-113 (station-voice SectionLabel kicker → SectionHeading + orange dot → meta row → closing hazard Separator tape) is the ONE surface-header family; every yesid surface head closes on hazard tape.
gaps.transit_state: MapHeadTitle.svelte:27-35 hand-rolls a .map-kicker + <h1> with a .map-dot and — critically — a text-shadow at :85 (glow-never-text law violation, dressed as 'legibility lift'). No signal head, no hazard tape, no corner meta. Freshness is bolted on separately (MapFreshness placement='head').
gaps.redo_spec: Rebuild MapHeadTitle as a compact horizontal Masthead-derived strip: SectionLabel variant='station' kicker ('RÉSEAU · EN DIRECT'), SectionHeading dot heading ('Carte en direct.'), the freshness folded into a meta slot, closed by a short hazard Separator. Kill the text-shadow — instead seat the title inside a small solid --surface-2 terminal lozenge (occlusion) so it survives over tiles WITHOUT text glow. Reuse Masthead's SectionLabel/SectionHeading/Separator so the map head reads byte-identical to every other surface head.

gaps.element: No schematic/draftsman ornament layer — the canvas has only a soft vignette where yesid frames heroes in full blueprint linework
gaps.yesid_evidence: ProjectsBlueprint.svelte:12-38 (BlueprintShell + tunneling DWG SVGs, DWG ref-labels 'SEC-PROJ / TUNNELING · DWG: TBM-EPB-SECTION · SCALE NTS | REV.B') and ManifestoEdgeLeft.svelte:18-27 (Beck-style route lines at --primary 14%, transit roundels) — the brand's texture layer.
gaps.transit_state: MapHero.svelte:1070 the only ornament is <div class='map-vignette'> — a plain edge fade. No linework, no scale marks, no DWG readout. The map — literally a transit map — carries none of the transit-draftsman schematic that defines the brand.
gaps.redo_spec: Add a pointer-events:none schematic frame layer over the canvas edges (NOT over the map center — respect the marks): Beck-style route linework in the top-left/bottom-right dead corners at color-mix(--primary 12%), plus a mono DWG ref-label strip ('DWG: STM-LIVE-NET · SCALE NTS · REV.LIVE') echoing ProjectsBlueprint's labels. This is a NEW map-local primitive (MapSchematicFrame) composing the Beck-line pattern from ManifestoEdgeLeft; it lives in the vignette's z-band. Reinforces 'same transit-draftsman brand' harder here than anywhere because the subject matter IS a transit diagram.

gaps.element: Canvas edges are unframed (bare vignette) — no CornerMarks tick frame, no coordinate CornerMeta readout
gaps.yesid_evidence: CornerMarks.svelte:29-34 (four blueprint corner ticks at --primary) and CornerMeta (lib/components/brand/CornerMeta.svelte) are the standard hero framing; Masthead.svelte:56-58,93-96 wires CornerMeta into every cornered head.
gaps.transit_state: The full-bleed canvas (MapHero.svelte:1055-1070) has no corner ticks and no coordinate meta. There is no draftsman frame telling you this is an instrument. Both CornerMarks and CornerMeta exist unused.
gaps.redo_spec: Drop <CornerMarks size='md' opacity=0.4> as an aria-hidden overlay inside .map-surface, and a <CornerMeta>-style mono coordinate readout pinned to one dead corner showing REAL live values: center lat/lon, current zoom, 'ÎLE DE MONTRÉAL', vehicle count ('617 TRACKED'). Wire it to the existing camera + live.vehicles reads (already reactive in MapHero). Honest-data only (CornerMeta's A4 rule). Gives the canvas the instrument frame yesid heroes wear.

gaps.transit_state: MapFreshness renders twice as detached pills (MapOverlayChrome.svelte:140 floating + :30 in head), plain text 'updated 24s ago'. No signal head, no prompt voice, no terminal chrome. The map's most brand-defining opportunity — showing LIVE data pulsing — is styled as a neutral timestamp.
gaps.redo_spec: Fold freshness into a single terminal-strip readout with the signal-head: StatusDot green pulse (fresh) / caution (stale) + a mono prompt line 'stm@live:vehicles> 617 tracked · 24s ago'. Either as the meta slot of the re-chassied title TerminalPanel, or a standalone mono strip using the signal-head markup from TerminalPanel:80-90. gaps.element: Detail panel opens as a generic RightPanel, not a terminal record readout
gaps.yesid_evidence: TerminalPanel.svelte:29-119 — a record surface = titlebar (signal head + title + tag + meta) → hazard stripe → body → mono footer of honest stats (n · window · generated_utc).

Selecting a bus should read like querying a record in the yesid terminal. Keep MapSelectionDetail's data logic; wrap its zones in the chassis + hazard stripe.

gaps.element: Filter/controls panel is an unframed floating div, not a titled terminal instrument
gaps.yesid_evidence: TerminalPanel titlebar grammar (:80-97) + yesid /projects left filter rail sits in framed terminal chrome (browser evidence: left filter rail with terminal-chrome preview grammar).

gaps.element: Numbered/wayfinding rhythm and MetroStation ornament are entirely absent from the map's legend and near-me results
gaps.yesid_evidence: Beck route roundels + numbered edge readouts (ManifestoEdgeLeft.svelte:33-49 sectionNumber/dot/readout rhythm); transit has NumberedChip and MetroStation primitives (lib/components/brand/) built for exactly this.
gaps.transit_state: Near-me nearby-stops list (MapNearMeControl.svelte:260-268) and the filter legend render as plain buttons/swatches with no numbered rhythm, no metro-station node ornament. The transit-brand wayfinding vocabulary is unused on the one surface most about wayfinding.

groups.notes: Trivia folded here: (1) MapHeadTitle.svelte:85 text-shadow is a live glow-never-text law concern — flagged inside gap 2; the fix (solid --surface-2 lozenge behind the title) removes the need for it. (2) The map imports NO brand-barrel primitives at all — the fastest structural tell that this surface was tokenized but never theatre-composed; every gap above is really one root cause (compose the brand chassis that already exists) sliced by panel. (3) All named primitives (TerminalPanel, CornerMarks, CornerMeta, Masthead, SectionLabel, StatusDot signal-head, NumberedChip, MetroStation, Separator hazard) are confirmed present in transit apps/web/src/lib/components/brand/ — no new primitive needed except MapSchematicFrame (gap 3), which composes the existing Beck-line pattern. (4) The 13-layer overlay law and full-bleed canvas law are NOT threatened by any of this — every proposal stays in the absolute-overlay z-band and touches no GL sizing. (5) Frozen dataviz marks (status/occupancy chip swatches, vehicle sprites, route linework) are untouched throughout.

gaps.element: Vertical edge-title column (the signature yesid listing chrome)
gaps.yesid_evidence: routes/[[lang=locale]]/projects/+layout.svelte:36-92 — Recipe 4 Edge Title Grid: grid-template-columns: auto 2px 1fr with a sticky edge-title-column (writing-mode: vertical-rl; transform: rotate(180deg); height:100dvh), giant heading font-size clamp(6rem,12vw,13rem)/weight 900/letter-spacing -0.04em, orange .edge-dot, metro-dots (station roundels) top+bottom, and a 2px primary accent-rail. Browser evidence: live /projects shows a giant vertical 'Projects.' edge title. Operator ruling 2 re-legalizes vertical edge-titles.
gaps.transit_state: Absent on all three. NetworkSurface.svelte:792-804 lays out .network-layout as grid 15rem/1fr (rail+content) with NO edge column; a grep for writing-mode:vertical / edge-title across transit lib+routes returns zero hits. Telling: NetworkSurface.svelte:776-779 still carries a vestigial comment + :global(.surface-shell.network){position:relative} 'Anchor for the D2 rotated edge word's zero-width absolute rail (it pins to the Surface's left gutter)' — the intent was specced (D2) and the anchor left in, but the element was never built.
gaps.redo_spec: Promote yesid's edge-title-column into a transit EdgeTitle brand primitive (lib/components/brand/EdgeTitle.svelte): a zero-width position:absolute rail pinned to the Surface left gutter (honor the existing .surface-shell.network anchor), display:none <1024px, writing-mode:vertical-rl + rotate(180deg), sticky top:0 height:100dvh. Props: text (the surface name from t.heading — 'RESEAU'/'NETWORK', 'POINTS CHAUDS'/'HOTSPOTS', 'RECIDIVISTES'), plus the top+bottom MetroStation dot clusters. groups.verdict_summary: The tokens, honest-absence, and the reliability §0-§4 rider-question arc are genuinely at parity — the reliability tab is the site's gold-standard story. But the DETAIL HEAD is where these three surfaces fall off a cliff versus yesid /projects/[slug]. yesid opens with a full-bleed manifesto hero: --manifesto color ground, dot-grid schematic, interactive canvas, cursor:crosshair, edge telemetry columns framing a display-scale glow title, then a 3-col body with a persistent stat rail. Transit's line/stop/trip heads are a plain flush-left text stack on the page background with three whisper-corner readouts hidden below 768px — a form label, not a masthead. The single biggest move: give all three surfaces the manifesto header band (DetailShell already ports it) with edge telemetry + a display-scale line-id title, then hang a persistent glance stat-rail beside the tabs and turn the §0 verdict into a living TerminalPanel readout rather than a bare banner.
keeps: Reliability §0-§4 internal arc (RouteReliabilityClusters + Section0-4): rider-question IA, plain-language two-sided verdict + OTP BAN, primary always-visible chart with progressive <Detail> disclosure — a real context→evidence→verdict spine (anatomy-line-detail §1b). Preserve as-is.

Honest-absence doctrine throughout: EdgeState emptyReason inference (RouteDetail.svelte:207-218,444-452), MaybeValue stand-downs (TripDetail.svelte:250-257,312-316), absentReason chips (StopDetail.svelte:503-509). Best-in-class; keep intact.
Deep-linkable ?tab mirror + metro station-signage active-tab chip (EntityDetail.svelte:142-155,241-246; RouteDetail.svelte:99-106) — shareable and on-brand. Keep.
Real-data-only telemetry primitives — TerminalPanel, CornerMeta, FreshnessStamp (StopDetail next board 317-330; cornerGeneratedStamp). Correct primitives, just under-deployed; keep and expand.

Colour/token discipline: --primary interactive-only, dataviz status fills on delay marks, four-color doctrine, no hex (RouteDetail delay tones 379-403; TripDetail data-tone 449-460). Substrate is correct.

groups.notes: Verdict on the re-seat question the task posed: do NOT fully re-seat line/stop onto DetailShell. EntityDetail's tabbed model is correct for these DATA DASHBOARDS (tab-switching Detail/Schedule/Reliability is the right IA); DetailShell's 3-col ToC+glance grid is built for long-scroll NARRATIVE (metrics/status). The right move is to LIFT DetailShell's three reusable pieces — the manifesto header band, the sticky glance/stat rail, and the sticky ToC — and hang them on the EntityDetail spine, rather than swapping spines. Trip is the exception: as an ephemeral single-column surface it can sit closer to a lighter Masthead-in-band. All gaps compose existing transit primitives (Masthead, TerminalPanel, CornerMarks, CornerMeta, TocNav/toc.ts, MetricDisplay, DetailShell band CSS) + existing tokens (--text-display, --manifesto, .detail-header-grid, --chrome-ink-opacity, --chrome-offset) — no new design tokens, no frozen-mark edits. Reliability §0-§4 stays untouched; the whole redo is HEAD + rail + verdict-frame chrome, exactly where the operator's 'tokens right, theatre wrong' verdict points.

groups.verdict_summary: Transit HEAD ported yesid's *primitives* faithfully (TerminalPanel, CornerMeta, Footer, hazard tape, tokens, four-color doctrine all at parity) but shipped almost none of the *theatre* those primitives were built to stage. Every yesid surface is a layered composition — a schematic BlueprintShell drawing behind, a rotated giant edge-title down the margin, GSAP scrub scenes drawing a metro line as you scroll, a full-bleed color-takeover thesis, glow-at-rest beams — and transit renders flat card-on-background boards instead. R2 must promote yesid's scrubs/ + MetroNetwork + BlueprintShell + edge-title grammar, then re-compose the home + listing surfaces on top of the kept substrate.
